Alright, so we have made it to the halfway meeting. Your challenges are three to four weeks into the challenge and now it's time to meet with them again and discuss their goals and discuss a few other things. So this is also called the abandoned hope in the sales section. So this lesson here is going to cover fulfilling the halfway meeting, not the sales training for it. Okay? So the sales training of this particular meeting, make sure you visit the sales section, the sales course, and this portal and it's under resell and ascension. Okay? So how to fulfill the halfway meeting. Step one is getting challengers to show up to the meeting. That's the number one step when it comes to fulfilling it is getting them there. So how do we do this? Well, the best way to do this is to schedule their halfway meeting on their weigh in day. Okay? As an example, these are best practices, but before they leave their orientation, so the first time you've ever met with them and they sign up for your challenge, make sure you schedule them for weigh in day. Okay? Now when they come to weigh in day and you go through the whole process, before they leave, make sure you schedule them for their halfway meeting. And again, when they come to their halfway meeting like they're at now and you meet with them, but before they leave, make sure you schedule their way out. That way they always have a standing appointment when they know that they're coming back to meet with you again. Then you are going to send them appointment reminders. I recommend sending a 48 hour and a two hour appointment reminder. Right? So two days before and then two hours before. That typically works really well for members. So once they show up and they are at the halfway meeting, the key is being prepared. Okay? So have whatever printouts, like maybe an in body sheet, their most recent one. Pictures, so weekly trackers, if they are sending you weekly trackers, weigh in results, visit reports, everything that you need to be able to truly assess them. Right? Because the point of the halfway meeting is taking a look at the original plan you guys created together based on their goals and you want to sit down and see where they're at. You know, they're at the halfway point. They're at the halfway point reaching their goals. Why or why not? Right? So you want to spend the first part of the conversation talking about that, but then having everything in front of you so it's not like speculation. You're looking at real tangible stats and data. Right? So make sure you have all of that stuff. I would take a look at your calendar for the day and see who you have for halfway meetings and then get all that stuff prepared in advance and just have them in different piles based on the clients that you're meeting with. You'll do this because you have about 20 to 30 minutes to talk with them about their goals, about their progress, and then pitch them on continuing to work with you. Okay? That's part of the sales training. But the conversation goes much better if you appear much more involved in their progress and you'll appear that way if you're prepared. Right? So almost all of us probably really care about the results that our clients get, but sometimes they could come off as you're not that prepared or care too much about their results because you just don't have anything there. You're just sitting there and a lot of people would expect to have like a really big, robust like halfway meeting like, okay, here we go. We're at the halfway point. What are we doing? And then they kind of just come in and there's nothing really there. Right? So this is for your clients. It doesn't mean you don't care. Right? But this is for your clients to show how much you care because you're extra prepared and you have everything laid out. Just them alone in their specific program and you're going to show them how you're going to crush the second half of it. So after you meet with them and you go through the sale, make sure to schedule their way out. Okay? And if you sold them something that you are giving them access to early, make sure you give it to them by end of day. Okay? Don't wait till the next day. This shows great fulfillment on your agreement because they just signed an agreement with you, but it also secures the contract. Right? So you can't give it back. So as an example, they joined your gym using the 42-day detox. That's the challenge that they're on. And at the halfway meeting, you agreed to give them the six-week challenge if they signed up early or whatever your abandoned hope sale says that you're giving them as a discount or as an early bird special or whatever it is, then give that to them right away because they can't give it back to you. Right? And they'll be, oh, you know what? I'm actually just going to go month to month and see how it goes. It's like, dude, I already gave you something that I normally charge $5.99 for. Like, you can't give it back. You already probably went through it. You know what I mean? So that's why you want to deliver it as soon as you can to them. Now, if they decide to not continue, make sure you have written notes as to why not. Right? Whether it's like, I got to talk to my husband or I have to do X, Y, and Z. Make sure that you have the notes on that so you can follow up with them on way out day when they show up to way out day and see if they still have those obstacles. See if you're able to overcome those objections on way out day. That's three more weeks they have to talk with their wife or that's three more weeks they have to think about it. Like you want to use that as fuel. That way when they come to way out day, you're like, hey, you know, we've had a few weeks to think about it. You know, I'm sure that you've played different scenarios. Like you've lost another 10 pounds. Like you look absolutely amazing. Let's make this thing happen. Right? So you don't want to forget about what you talked about at the halfway meeting and basically have to start all over from scratch. And depending on how many members you have, you know, it might be hard to remember what everybody says at their halfway meetings. That's why you want to write it down and take great notes. And then those are the same people you'll be seeing a few weeks later at way out day. So hopefully that helps. That is the process to the halfway meeting. This meeting should be happening week four. So after they've finished three meetings on week four, you're going to schedule a 30 minute time with all of your challengers to sit down and talk about continuing with you. And you'll go over all the stuff that's in the sales play. And then the next thing that you'll be doing is way out. So let's jump into the way out fulfillment now.
